title: rubus plicatus var. contiguus
intro: Rubus plicatus var. contiguus, also known as the clustered thimbleberry, is a variety of the thimbleberry plant, a type of bramble that belongs to the rose family. The plant has been found to have medicinal properties, particularly in its use as a natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent, due to its high content of salicylic acid. The health benefits of Rubus plicatus var. contiguus include its ability to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as its potential to aid in the treatment of digestive issues and skin conditions. The plant can be prepared for medicinal use through various herbal preparations, such as infusions, tinctures, and salves.
